mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with declining unemployment signals tight labor market conditions that drive corporate pricing power and earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between PAYEMS/UNRATE and SPX returns falls below 0.15 in 60-day rolling windows, or interaction terms show no significant predictive power (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer demand, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days due to reduced economic activity expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST declines by 5%+ over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production relative to its 12-month moving average indicates expanding supply capacity, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days due to deflationary pressures.
failure_condition: SPX returns exceed 1.5% in 60-day windows where INDPRO grows by 2%+ YoY, contradicting the predicted negative relationship.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in CPI above 240 signals persistent inflation, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days due to expected monetary tightening.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) and GDP growth (GDPC1) predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days due to deteriorating macroeconomic fundamentals.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS/GDPC1 fails to predict negative SPX returns in 40%+ of cases over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in unemployment rate above 4.5% signals weakening labor market conditions that predict negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days due to reduced consumer spending expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in industrial production below its 6-month moving average indicates contracting supply capacity, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days due to inventory adjustments and price rebounds.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO falls below 95% of its 6-month moving average.
